Overview
The AI Agents: Intensive Vibe Coding Capstone Project invites participants to build an AI agent using the concepts, tools, and best practices learned in Kaggle’s 5-Day AI Agents: Intensive Vibe Coding Course with Google. Participants will create a practical, real-world solution that demonstrates how AI agents can automate tasks, assist users, solve business challenges, or improve everyday workflows.

Submissions should showcase both the project's value and technical implementation through a Kaggle Writeup, public codebase, video demonstration, and project link. Projects may be entered into one of four tracks: Agents for Good, Agents for Business, Concierge Agents, or Freestyle and will be evaluated based on innovation, solution design, communication, and the effective application of course concepts and agent technologies.

Description
AI agents are rapidly changing how we interact with technology, enabling systems that can reason, take action, and complete complex tasks on behalf of users. In this capstone project, you'll apply the concepts, tools, and techniques learned throughout Kaggle’s 5-Day AI Agents: Intensive Vibe Coding Course with Google to build an agent that solves a meaningful real-world problem.
Whether you're creating an assistant that helps individuals stay organized, streamlines business processes, supports social impact initiatives, or explores a completely new idea, this project is an opportunity to move beyond experimentation and develop something useful, practical, and shareable. We encourage participants to think creatively, focus on delivering value, and demonstrate how agent-based systems can address real challenges.

Your submission should showcase both the vision behind your project and the technical decisions that bring it to life. "# AI Agent Overlay & Skills Implementation Plan

## Goal Description
Build a Chrome extension that overlays the browser, showing a preview of all tasks performed by the AI agent and allowing the user to revise them. Implement a suite of agent **skills** for corporate email handling, statistical visualization from Excel sheets, meeting management, CI/CD pipeline creation, test execution, and email security. The solution will use a Python backend for heavy processing and a JavaScript frontend for the extension UI.

## User Review Required
> [!IMPORTANT] 
> The architecture involves a **browser extension** (Chrome) and a **backend server** (Python). Ensure you are comfortable hosting a backend (e.g., on a cloud VM or using a service like Render). The Chrome extension will need permissions to read page content and communicate with the backend via HTTPS.

> [!WARNING] 
> The email security skill uses a **custom ML model** for phishing detection, which will require training data and periodic updates. Clarify if you have suitable datasets or prefer to start with a smaller rule‑based baseline.

## Open Questions
- Do you have a domain or subdomain to host the backend API over HTTPS? (Required for Chrome extension communication)
- Which cloud provider (if any) would you like to use for deployment?
- Should the ML model be hosted locally (Docker) or as a managed service?
- Do you need persistent storage for user‑specific data (e.g., revision history) – if so, preferred DB (SQLite, Postgres)?

## Proposed Changes
---
### Frontend – Chrome Extension
- **[NEW] manifest.json** – declares permissions (activeTab, storage, scripting, https://<your‑api-domain>/*) and background scripts.
- **[NEW] background.js** – manages message passing between content scripts and the backend.
- **[NEW] content_script.js** – injects an overlay UI onto any page, displays task list, and opens a revision modal.
- **[NEW] popup.html / popup.js** – UI for quick access to agent controls.
- **[NEW] styles.css*
